@@ -1,7 +1,30 @@
 def parse_host_port(value: str, default_port: int = 80) -> tuple[str, int]:
+    if not value:
+        return value, default_port
+    # Handle bracketed IPv6 addresses like "[::1]:8080"
+    if value[0] == "[":
+        end_idx = value.find(']')
+        if end_idx == -1:
+            # No closing bracket; fall back to default behaviour
+            return value, default_port
+        host = value[1:end_idx]
+        rest = value[end_idx + 1:]
+        if rest.startswith(':'):
+            port_text = rest[1:]
+            if port_text:
+                try:
+                    port = int(port_text)
+                except ValueError:
+                    port = default_port
+            else:
+                port = default_port
+        else:
+            port = default_port
+        return host, port
+    # Default handling for normal host:port formats
     host, sep, port_text = value.partition(":")
     if not sep:
-        return value, default_port
+        return host, default_port
     if port_text == "":
         return host, default_port
     return host, int(port_text)
"
